title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen identifies a recurring "T" motif at the crime scene, noting the T-shaped totem pole, the victim's decapitated and outstretched body, and the T-shaped blood smear. He compares these elements to a murder that occurred in West Virginia six months prior, where a victim was nailed to a T-shaped crossroads sign and a similar blood smear was found. Detective Vane identifies a man named Harakat, a man described as a lunatic who runs a nudist camp on Oyster Island.

Ellery realizes that clues associated with Wylie Crosette are reappearing, suggesting this crime may be a sequel to a previous murder. While Adair suggests the T-shaped motifs and crucifixions link the crime to Harakat, Ellery notes the existence of a mysterious figure named Croscius. Ellery mentions that Harakat, who is described as a "sun god" figure, is the only person who can confirm Croscius's existence. Vance reports that although Oyster Island is under surveillance, it currently poses no immediate danger.

The group enters a colonial-style mansion to meet the bereaved, encountering Margaret Braid, her daughter Helen Breda, and Jonah Lincoln. During a somber meeting, Eames asks Lincoln to explain the circumstances regarding the tickets from the previous night. Lincoln explains that Thomas Braid had called him to say he had obtained Broadway tickets for Mrs. Braid, Helen, and Lincoln, and had instructed Lincoln to take the ladies out while he went home.
